The “interviews you first” approach is interesting. SEO agents often jump straight into execution, but the quality of the initial diagnosis probably determines whether the recommendations are actually useful. Curious to see how you handle cases where the founder’s stated SEO problem isn’t the real bottleneck.

@aryan787544 Great question, and worth clarifying a little about the purpose of the interview. It's less about being an SEO diagnosis of your content, and more of a way to pull out your first-hand material, the opinion and numbers that make a post worth reading. [...] Some key features:

 💬 Chat driven — accessible via web chat / Telegram even on your phone!
 🎙️ Interview mode — conducts an interview on the topic to understand your perspective and build the basis for the post
 🔍 Deep Research — finds supporting facts and research backing your point of view
 🗓️ Keyword research & content calendar
 📊 Diagrams — visually represent concepts
 🎨 Brand Tone — learns your voice from reference material
 ✍️ Humanizer — reduces the chance of being flagged as AI writing
 🚧 Approval Gates — makes sure human checks are in place when needed
 🚀 Auto-publish to Shopify and WordPress
 🧠 Knowledge Base — everything you do on the platform feeds an ever-improving repository of your business context

results.title: ACME.BOT: AI Blog Agent That Interviews You
results.content:
LoginSign up

# Your blog on autopilot. Written with your expertise.

ACME.BOT is an AI blog agent that runs your blog from research to publish. It interviews you first, so every post carries the take only you have.

Start free trial

no credit card100 free creditsno sales call

Abhishek Iyer, maker of ACME.BOT

Abhishek Iyer

Made by anex-Google Search engineer.

“Running experiments on how search engines rank is my bread and butter, so when I couldn't find a tool that used AI to write genuinely helpful content, I built my own.”

as featured on

Featured on New York MagazineFeatured on The Information

Most AI blogs sound the same.

They read like an average of Wikipedia.

Readers scroll past. Search engines bury them.

Yours won't.

Most AI blogs read like Wikipedia. Yours won't. [...] Most AI blogs read like Wikipedia. Yours won't.

Most AI blogsread likeWikipedia.Yours won't.

## Why would any answer engine rely on a page it could have produced itself?

Google has a name for what survives.NON-COMMODITY CONTENT.

 Experience: first-hand, lived detail
 Expertise: your knowledge, cited
 Authoritativeness: sourced, fact-checked
 Trust: transparent + reviewed

Built around Google's E-E-A-T by an ex-Google search engineer.

A hand-lettered cardboard sign reading 'no AI slop', with a crossed-out symbol

HOW IT WORKS

## ACME.BOT interviews you before it writes.

It does the work. You just weigh in with the take only you have.

1

Researches the web

Deep research on the topic, competitors, and what's already ranking.

2

Reads your world [...] Shopify

Shopify

 Native blog integration
 Product linking within articles
 SEO fields auto-populated
 Collection-aware internal links
 Supports Shopify's blog structure

FAQ

## Before you ask.

Will it actually sound like me?

That's the whole point. Before it writes, the agent interviews you in chat and builds the piece around your answers: your takes, your stories, your voice. It's not a generic draft with your name on it.

How is this different from ChatGPT or other AI writers?

Those write an average of what already ranks: generic pages an answer engine could produce itself. ACME.BOT does the research, keywords, and SEO, then interviews you for the opinions, stories, and experience only you have. Google calls the result non-commodity content.

How much of my time does it need?

results.title: Do AI SEO Agents actually work? Looking for real results
results.content: can generate itself). Most automation fall short here. Its super easy to get AI to churn out thousands of posts. However it is tricky, but possible to create an AI agent that captures the experience and expertise of a human-in-the-loop and that does the "right thing". ACME.BOT tries to do this my employing various human-in-the-loop mechanics (interview mode, knowledge base, approval systems). [...] - Before you read - disclaimer - ex-Google search engineer and founder of ACME.BOT here. I know this is an old thread - but wanted to drop a note that since this post several changes have happened: Yes, some AI content was indeed ranking for a brief period Most of them got killed by Google updates that aggressively penalized scaled AI content. The phenomenon was so widespread, that there is a term for this now - its called "Mount AI" (meteoric rise of AI content traffic followed by a drop) Google has cleaned up its directive about AI content. It is now quite clear and unambiguous - Google will rank "non-commodity" content - ie. content that Google's own LLM can't produce (after all what use does Google have for AI content that it can generate itself). Most automation fall short here. Its

results.title: Brand Knowledge Base for AI Content | ACME.BOT
results.content:
# Your expertise. Every article.

Your docs, web clips, and interview answers become ACME.BOT's source of truth. Every article cites your exact specs, names, and numbers, not generic Wikipedia-sounding AI slop.Your docs, clips, and interview answers become ACME.BOT's source of truth. Every article cites your specs, names, and numbers, not Wikipedia-sounding AI slop.

Knowledge base panel showing uploaded documents categorized by type

GROUNDED CONTENT

### Stop summarizing the internet.

Generic AI writes the internet's consensus: summaries any competitor can churn out. Knowledge Base fills in what's missing — your data, your frameworks, and the answers you gave in the interview — with inline citations.

An article citing the knowledge base inline: 8 citations, 3 of them from your KB. [...] The ACME.BOT browser extension clipping a competitor pricing teardown into the Research category, saved with tags.

## What you say. How you say it.

### Brand Voice

### Brand Knowledge

Knowledge Base handles substance. The AI Humanizer handles voice. You get both.

## Before you ask.

Still have questions? Contact us

## Footer

AI tools and agents to automate content marketing.

human@acme.bot

### Tools

### Features

### Guides

### Company

© 2026 ACME.BOT All rights reserved. [...] RESEARCH ARCHIVE

### Put your research to work.

Industry reports, customer interviews, teardowns: documents you haven't opened in months, buried in folders. Upload once. Every article pulls the right slice, quoted and cited.

The knowledge base research category, with an interviews document highlighted.

FEEDBACK

### Say it once. It sticks.

Correct a draft and the correction doesn't die in that thread. It's saved as a permanent rule and applied to every draft after it, so you're never fixing the same thing twice.

Feedback saved as active style rules in ACME.BOT, marked as applied to future drafts
